kaos_elements.name: Safety Goal
kaos_elements.description: Safety objective for ATM: A bank client can have two types of accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): A bank client can have two types of accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (fault tolerance): A checking-account and a saving-account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for fault tolerance

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (risk mitigation): For each checking account, one related saving-account can exists
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for risk mitigation

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (hazard prevention): The application must verify that a client can gain access to his or her account by identification via a personal identification number (PIN) code
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for hazard prevention

kaos_elements.name: Safety Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure safety (fault tolerance): Neither a checking-account nor a saving-account can have a negative balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for fault tolerance

steps.analysis_text: Round 1: SafetyAgent submits model for dialectic review by EfficiencyAgent.
steps.analysis: Round 1: SafetyAgent submits model for dialectic review by EfficiencyAgent.

steps.analysis_text: The negotiation between SafetyAgent and EfficiencyAgent reveals overlapping requirements regarding the handling of checking and saving accounts. Both agents have goals that emphasize the necessity of maintaining account integrity and ensuring user access through secure methods. However, there are potential conflicts in prioritization, especially regarding how transactions are processed in relation to account balances.
steps.analysis: The negotiation between SafetyAgent and EfficiencyAgent reveals overlapping requirements regarding the handling of checking and saving accounts. Both agents have goals that emphasize the necessity of maintaining account integrity and ensuring user access through secure methods. However, there are potential conflicts in prioritization, especially regarding how transactions are processed in relation to account balances.
steps.feedback: Further discussions are required to align the safety and efficiency perspectives, particularly on the handling of overdrafts and transaction approvals. It may be beneficial to prioritize user experience while ensuring safety protocols are not compromised.

steps.analysis_text: The negotiation process has identified several safety and efficiency requirements related to account management in the banking application. While there are some overlaps in the requirements regarding account types and balance checks, all elements are currently in a state that requires attention and updates. There are pending validations for efficiency goals that align closely with safety goals, particularly regarding account access and balance management.
steps.analysis: The negotiation process has identified several safety and efficiency requirements related to account management in the banking application. While there are some overlaps in the requirements regarding account types and balance checks, all elements are currently in a state that requires attention and updates. There are pending validations for efficiency goals that align closely with safety goals, particularly regarding account access and balance management.
steps.feedback: It is crucial to ensure that all safety and efficiency requirements are aligned and validated to prevent any conflicts during implementation. The overlapping requirements should be reviewed to streamline the process and confirm that they serve both safety and efficiency objectives effectively.

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Goal
kaos_elements.description: Efficiency objective for ATM: For each checking account, one related saving-account can exists
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (latency optimization): For each checking account, one related saving-account can exists
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for latency optimization

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (throughput stability): The application must verify that a client can gain access to his or her account by identification via a personal identification number (PIN) code
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for throughput stability

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (resource utilization): Neither a checking-account nor a saving-account can have a negative balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for resource utilization

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (latency optimization): The application should automatically withdraw funds from a related saving-account if the requested withdrawal amount on the checking-account is more than its current balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for latency optimization

kaos_elements.name: Efficiency Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure efficiency (throughput stability): If the saving-account balance is insufficient to cover the requested withdrawal amount, the application should inform the user and terminate the transaction
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for throughput stability

kaos_elements.name: Sustainability Goal
kaos_elements.description: Sustainability objective for ATM: Neither a checking-account nor a saving-account can have a negative balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Sustainability Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure sustainability (energy footprint reduction): Neither a checking-account nor a saving-account can have a negative balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for energy footprint reduction

kaos_elements.name: Sustainability Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure sustainability (resource lifecycle control): The application should automatically withdraw funds from a related saving-account if the requested withdrawal amount on the checking-account is more than its current balance
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for resource lifecycle control

kaos_elements.name: Sustainability Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure sustainability (environmental impact awareness): If the saving-account balance is insufficient to cover the requested withdrawal amount, the application should inform the user and terminate the transaction
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for environmental impact awareness

kaos_elements.name: Sustainability Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure sustainability (energy footprint reduction): The bank client must be able to deposit an amount to and withdraw an amount from his or her accounts using the bank application
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for energy footprint reduction

kaos_elements.name: Sustainability Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure sustainability (resource lifecycle control): Each transaction must be recorded, and the client must have the ability to review all transactions performed against a given account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for resource lifecycle control

kaos_elements.name: Sustainability Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure sustainability (environmental impact awareness): Recorded transactions must include the date, time, transaction type, amount and account balance after the transaction
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for environmental impact awareness

kaos_elements.name: Sustainability Requirement 7
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure sustainability (energy footprint reduction): A bank client can have two types of accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for energy footprint reduction

steps.analysis_text: Round 1: GreenAgent submits model for dialectic review by TrustworthinessAgent.
steps.analysis: Round 1: GreenAgent submits model for dialectic review by TrustworthinessAgent.

steps.analysis_text: The requirements for both sustainability and trustworthiness align closely, emphasizing the importance of maintaining a positive account balance, recording transactions, and ensuring the client can deposit and withdraw funds. However, there are several overlapping themes that could benefit from refinement to avoid redundancy.
steps.analysis: The requirements for both sustainability and trustworthiness align closely, emphasizing the importance of maintaining a positive account balance, recording transactions, and ensuring the client can deposit and withdraw funds. However, there are several overlapping themes that could benefit from refinement to avoid redundancy.
steps.feedback: It is recommended to clarify the distinctions between sustainability and trustworthiness requirements, particularly those that focus on transaction recording and account management. This will enhance the clarity of the system's objectives and improve user understanding.

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Goal
kaos_elements.description: Trustworthiness objective for ATM: If the saving-account balance is insufficient to cover the requested withdrawal amount, the application should inform the user and terminate the transaction
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (security assurance): If the saving-account balance is insufficient to cover the requested withdrawal amount, the application should inform the user and terminate the transaction
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for security assurance

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (auditability): The bank client must be able to deposit an amount to and withdraw an amount from his or her accounts using the bank application
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for auditability

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (integrity guarantees): Each transaction must be recorded, and the client must have the ability to review all transactions performed against a given account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for integrity guarantees

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (security assurance): Recorded transactions must include the date, time, transaction type, amount and account balance after the transaction
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for security assurance

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (auditability): A bank client can have two types of accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for auditability

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (integrity guarantees): A checking-account and a saving-account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for integrity guarantees

kaos_elements.name: Trustworthiness Requirement 7
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure trustworthiness (security assurance): For each checking account, one related saving-account can exists
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for security assurance

steps.analysis_text: Round 1: TrustworthinessAgent submits model for dialectic review by ResponsibilityAgent.
steps.analysis: Round 1: TrustworthinessAgent submits model for dialectic review by ResponsibilityAgent.

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Goal
kaos_elements.description: Responsibility objective for ATM: Each transaction must be recorded, and the client must have the ability to review all transactions performed against a given account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Quality-attribute specific requirement analysis

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 1
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (regulatory accountability): Each transaction must be recorded, and the client must have the ability to review all transactions performed against a given account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for regulatory accountability

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 2
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (stakeholder transparency): Recorded transactions must include the date, time, transaction type, amount and account balance after the transaction
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for stakeholder transparency

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 3
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (ethical compliance): A bank client can have two types of accounts
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for ethical compliance

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 4
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (regulatory accountability): A checking-account and a saving-account
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for regulatory accountability

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 5
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (stakeholder transparency): For each checking account, one related saving-account can exists
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for stakeholder transparency

kaos_elements.name: Responsibility Requirement 6
kaos_elements.description: The system shall ensure responsibility (ethical compliance): The application must verify that a client can gain access to his or her account by identification via a personal identification number (PIN) code
kaos_elements.measurable_criteria: Clause-level quality requirement for ethical compliance
